theme.name: The Workshop
theme.setting_summary: A training campaign. One mechanic per puzzle, taught by a mentor in plain language.

acts.title: The Apprentice's Manual
acts.intro_text:
Welcome to the Workshop. You'll learn one operator at a time. Take your time — there's no clock other than the one in the puzzle.

When you're ready, choose 'First Flow' below.
acts.outro_text:
You've worked through the manual. The mentor sets down their tools and gestures at the wider workshop.

"You're ready for commissions of your own now. Real ones — the kind whose shape no one knows ahead of time."

puzzles.title: First Flow
puzzles.briefing:
Mentor: "Every flow has an origin and a destination. The conveyor is the simplest operator — it carries what arrives, in the direction it points.

Drop three conveyors between the input on the left and the output on the right. Run, watch."

puzzles.title: The Branching Path
puzzles.briefing:
Mentor: "The splitter sends arrivals alternately along its two perpendicular outputs. First one way, then the other, on every arrival.

There are two outputs this time. Use one splitter to feed both."

puzzles.title: Two Hands, One Mind
puzzles.briefing:
Mentor: "When no tile fits the bend, place an operator. You'll trace where they walk — a path of cells — and write a tiny program of operations they cycle through.

GRAB picks up flow at their cell. DROP releases it. MOVE walks one step along the path. Their hands hold one thing at a time.

The input here is at the left. No tiles are allowed in this puzzle — only the operator. Have them walk back and forth."

puzzles.title: The Sorter's Eye
puzzles.briefing:
Mentor: "The filter lets one kind of flow through and blocks the rest. Wrong-type flow piles up on the filter cell — that's normal; it just means you've chosen not to route it onward.

The input now produces two kinds of flow, alternately. Only alpha should reach the output."

puzzles.title: Confluence
puzzles.briefing:
Mentor: "Two flows arrive from different directions; the merger gathers them and passes them down its facing line.

There are two inputs above and below. Route them both to the same output through a single merger."

puzzles.title: Graduation: The First Commission
puzzles.briefing:
Mentor: "This is what the work looks like at scale.

Two inputs of different flow. A reactor that combines them into a third kind. Route the third kind to the output.

Everything you've learned, working together. Take your time."

ending.good:
You leave the Workshop with the tools you came for: a knowledge of flow, of operators, of the small programs that bend one into another. The mentor's voice stays with you in some shape, even after the doors close behind you.

— The Apprentice's Manual, complete.
ending.neutral: The work is done, for now.
